title: Twitter @AmelieTabatta
summary:
But! Even more exciting: you don't even need the full 400+ GH200-hour pipeline. A supervised + KD combo gets you more than 99% of the gains at ~10× less compute.
Stop skipping the supervised contrastive step before distillation.

title: Twitter @antoine_chaffin
summary:
@din0s_ this one was _just above_ SOTA because we used old data
Let me remind the public datasets we built with the help of IR avengers
The open source community has not received all its gifts just yet
I won't stop until all the leaderboards are topped by PyLate boilerplates

title: Twitter @antoine_chaffin
summary:

title: Twitter @antoine_chaffin
summary:
Now that we showed that we can reach SOTA with non-SOTA data, what would happen if we ran these training with stronger data?
Guess we'll find out very soon 👀

title: Twitter @antoine_chaffin
summary:
Most ColBERT models only do phase 3 on top of models that performed the first two phases in a single vector setting
But is this really enough?
To explore this, we leverage the well known work of Nomic Embed and compare different setups
